title: Mandatory Returns and Unroutable Publication · Language Lens: Unroutable Failure Policy
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must continuously consume return notifications and correlate them without assuming cross-channel event ordering; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the unroutable failure policy increment in a correlated Go return and route-evidence ledger. Learners progress thr…
objectives: Classify unroutable messages as contract, deployment, tenant, or malicious failures and choose alert, quarantine, reject, or repair ownership; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Build topology fixtures that assert expected destination sets, forbidden routes, unroutable cases, permissions, and changed bindings; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
